title: Sweep vs genuine breakout: measurable rule = close back inside the swept level within 1-3 bars
claim: A liquidity sweep is distinguished from a genuine breakout by whether price closes back inside the swept level within 1-3 bars (with a long wick beyond the level and small body); vendor claims 65-75% win rate and 25-35% failure rate for confirmed sweep-and-reverse setups, but discloses no methodology.

source.label: CrossTrade — Liquidity Sweeps (price action education)
source.quote: Within 1-3 bars, price closes back below (above) the swept level ... a very long wick in the swept direction and a small body — a classic rejection candle. ... A sweep of a swing high in a strong uptrend is more likely to be a real breakout than a sweep-and-reverse.

rule_formal: Sweep(level L, up-side): High(t) > L AND Close(t+k) < L for some k in {0,1,2} (close back inside within 1-3 bars); else Break. Confirmation quality: wick beyond L >> body. Context veto: strong pre-existing trend in sweep direction -> treat as breakout, not sweep.

numbers.effect: claimed win rate 65-75%, avg RR 2:1 to 4:1, failure rate 25-35% for confirmed sweep-and-reverse
numbers.sample: not disclosed — no n, no period, no instrument
numbers.caveats: numbers are unattributed vendor claims with zero methodology — capture value is the operational 1-3 bar close-back-inside definition, not the win rate; the trend-context veto is consistent with our EMA-slope filter

mechanism: Stops resting beyond equal highs / obvious levels get filled into the hands of larger sellers; once that liquidity is consumed and price re-enters the range, the swept breakout entrants are trapped and must exit back through the level.
relevance: Q4 direct (first measurable sweep-vs-break rule: penetration + close-back-inside window at Asian high/low), Q1 (the same rule inverted = breakout confirmation: entry only if no close back inside within k bars)

test_design.lookahead_risk: waiting 1-3 bars after penetration delays entry by k bars — measurable at entry but shifts entry price; must test cost of delayed fill vs false-break avoided

overlap_risk: none of the five falsified variables measure post-penetration behavior; nearest system component is the breakout entry itself — this modifies entry timing rather than day selection
